content.full_text:
SMUD
SACRAMENTO MUNICIPAL UTILITY DISTRICT □ P.O. Box 15830, Sacramento CA 95852-1830, (916) 452-3211
AN ELECTRIC SYSTEM SERVING THE HEART OF CALIFORNIA

JAN 22 10 04 AM '90

LEG 90-059

January 19, 1990


Cathy Donovan, Legal Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95807

Re: Application of Government Code Section 87200
to a Municipal Utility District

Dear Ms. Donovan:

Pursuant to a conversation with Colleen McGee of your
staff on January 18, 1989, I am writing to request an advice letter
which addresses the following question.

Does the 1989 amendment to section 87200
of the Government Code, effective January 1,
1990, which included county treasurers, city
treasurers, and "other public officials who
manage public investments" for disclosure
purposes, apply to the treasurer and other
officials of a Municipal Utility District who
manage public investments?

A Municipal Utility District ("District") is a public
agency, formed pursuant to, and governed by, the Municipal Utility
District Act, Section 11501, et seq. of the Public Utilities Code.
Its function is to provide a utility service to customers within
its service area boundaries.

A District is under the direction of a Board of Directors
elected by a majority vote of the people of the ward in which the
Director is elected. It is not a branch of city, county, or state
government, although a District is a political subdivision of the
State.

It appears from the interim regulations which have been
provided to define what is meant by "manage public investments" that
the emphasis is placed on "employees and consultants who have
decision making authority with regard to investment of city,
county, or state funds." Although District revenues from rates
charged to our customers and the return on the investment of
surplus revenues are public funds, they are strictly District
public funds and not city, county, or state funds.


DISTRICT HEADQUARTERS □ 6201 S Street, Sacramento CA 95817-1899

Cathy Donovan                    -2-                    January 19, 1990

     As advised by Colleen McGee, the Sacramento Municipal
Utility District will not attempt to comply with the disclosure
requirements of Government Code Section 87200 regarding employees
who manage public investments, until receipt of an advice letter
from you directing that compliance. It is also understood that the
Sacramento Municipal Utility District will not be subject to
penalties for failure to comply with filing deadlines for forms
721 and if required to comply, would be given a reasonable time to
do so after such direction.

     If I can supply you with any further information which
may be helpful to you in addressing the foregoing question, please
do not hesitate to contact the undersigned at 732-6126. Thank you
very much for your assistance in this matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Dana Appling
                                        Attorney

sections.question: Does the 1989 amendment to section 87200 of the Government Code, effective January 1, 1990, which included county treasurers, city treasurers, and "other public officials who manage public investments" for disclosure purposes, apply to the treasurer and other officials of a Municipal Utility District who manage public investments?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the 1989 amendment to Government Code Section 87200 requiring disclosure by public officials managing public investments applies to officials of a Municipal Utility District managing District public funds.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion section is present. The request references a prior conversation with FPPC staff. Document appears to be the original request from the District attorney seeking guidance before complying with disclosure requirements.

embedding.summary: Sacramento Municipal Utility District requests an FPPC advice letter regarding whether the 1989 amendment to Government Code Section 87200 applies to District treasurers and officials managing public investments, arguing that District funds are not city, county, or state funds.
